meta.title: Life Skills & Independent Living | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Everyday habits and routines that build independence—skills, confidence, and choices.

hero.title: Life Skills & Independent Living Preparation
hero.subtitle: Everyday skills for confidence and independence—routines, home tasks, money basics, and community navigation.
hero.bestFor: Young adults preparing for more independence at home and in the community.

blocks.html: Life Skills & Independent Living Preparation focuses on the everyday habits and routines that make independence possible. It's not about doing everything alone; it's about building skills, confidence, and choices.

items: Using calendars, alarms, or visual schedules
Simple meal prep and kitchen safety
Handling cash and cards in real or practice scenarios
Practicing purchases in real stores when appropriate
Using rides with family, paratransit, or rideshare safely
Learning routes to familiar places (library, rec center, etc.)
Managing frustration and asking for help
Improved comfort in stores and community spaces

sessionStyle.title: Session Style
items: Goal-driven supports tied to daily life

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to talk goals and supports for daily independence?

blocks.html: Families can schedule recurring sessions where a mentor works alongside the participant on practical, goal-driven skills.

Life Skills & Independent Living sessions are individualized supports funded through DDD Goods & Services. Focus areas include daily routines, simple meal preparation, basic money skills, and community navigation. Supports are practical, goal-driven, and designed to increase the person's independence and participation at home and in the community.
